title: Hieracium cuspidellum
intro: Hieracium cuspidellum, also known as Hawkweed or Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium cuspidellum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in some herbal remedies to treat wounds, skin conditions, and as a natural antiseptic.
taxonomy: Hieracium cuspidellum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium cuspidellum is placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae and tribe Senecioneae. The species is further classified into the subgenus Pilosella, which is a group of related Hieracium species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics.
